summary: Leymebamba highland khipu (88 cords, 6 STRING / 70 INT / 12 EMPTY) at the Centro Mallqui (Laguna de los Condores collection), Amazonas, Peru. Chachapoyas culture, late Inca period (~1400-1532 CE). Architecture 1-PAIRE STRUCTURED to depth L3. 11 clusters with massive numerical magnitudes (peak values 2565, 2018, 1796, 1782, 1473, 1422, 1290 across closing clusters 8-9) -- tribute / census register-scale. Colors: AB 20, MB 17, KB 16, W 15, GG 11, plus minor BL / RL / MB. EXTRAORDINARY STRUCTURAL FEATURE: all 6 STRING cords are BURIED AT DEPTH L3 as grandchildren of a SINGLE L2 subsidiary (on L1 cord 7, W color, val=759 -> L2 AB INT 13 -> 7 L3 children including 6 STRING cords). The L3 narrative cell reads: chiqaqaqa-GEN / qaqaqaqaqaqa / qaqaqaqaqaqa-DIR / maqa chi-DIR / qaqaqaqa-1OBJ / TOP-chi-1OBJ. 3 of 6 STRING cords carry the -1OBJ suffix (-wa = 'me') -- the SAME oracular personal-voice signature as UR055 (Hair Khipu, ritual_oracle singleton, Pachacamac). UR015 proposes the SECOND ritual_oracle in the validated corpus, but structurally inverted: where UR055 is DOMINANT text across 180 cords (94 percent LK), UR015 is EMBEDDED at L3 inside a large numerical register. Proposed structural variant: 'embedded ritual oracle' -- a divinatory consultation cell buried in a tribute / census document, consistent with the Laguna de los Condores tomb-cave context where ancestor consultation was practiced at burial sites. v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE (STRING onsets are L2 chi-, E qa-, L3 ma- -- none are L10 / L11).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-17)
  88 cords total. Architecture 1-PAIRE STRUCTURED (depth L3).
  11 clusters of varying sizes [1, 1, 3, 3, 31, 4, 8, 2, 7, 4, 1].
  Composition: INT 70 (80 percent), STRING 6, EMPTY 12.
  Provenance: Leymebamba, Amazonas, Peru. Museum: Centro Mallqui, Leymebamba (CMA-628/LC1-257F).
  Context: the Centro Mallqui houses the LAGUNA DE LOS CONDORES collection -- 32 khipus discovered in 1996-97 in a cliff-tomb complex of Chachapoyas culture mummy bundles, dated late Inca period (~1400-1532 CE). The khipus are attributed to the Chachapoyas ayllus administered under Tupac Yupanqui's and Huayna Capac's conquests.
  Colors: AB (anonymous beige) 20, MB (medium brown) 17, KB (black-blue) 16, W (white) 15, GG (gray) 11. Minor BL / RL / MB ornamental colors. No LK (text color), no RM (ritual color), no BG (identity).
  KFG twin KH0251.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  6/6 STRING cords readable via compound morphology analysis; 3/6 have full dict glosses (qaqa and maqa are attested; the multi-qa chains are morphological reduplications not in the base dictionary).
  50 percent base-dict hit rate as reported by reader (3 of 6 on plain vocabulary). Compound readings are structural.

ASCHER LAYER
  0 pendant-pendant sums declared. No Ascher graph available. LOW / NONE integrity tier.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  The CRITICAL observation is the placement of all 6 STRING cords:
    L1 cord 7 (cluster 3, W color, val 759, knots S100 E S100 E S100 S100 S100 S100 L9 approximately) has
    L2 subsidiary (AB color, val 13, INT) which has
    7 L3 grandchildren: 6 STRING + 1 INT.
  No STRING cords anywhere else in the 88-cord document. The entire textual content is localized to this single L2 -> L3 branch.
  The 6 STRING L3 cords form a SEQUENCE:
    L3 cord 1: 'L2 E E E L6' = chiqaqaqa-GEN ('of the triple-truth-rock', morphology: chi- onset + qa+qa+qa + -pa GEN)
    L3 cord 2: 'E E E E E E' = qaqaqaqaqaqa ('sixfold rock', pure repetition of figure-eight)
    L3 cord 3: 'E E E E E E L3' = qaqaqaqaqaqa-DIR ('toward the sixfold rock', +ma DIR)
    L3 cord 4: 'L3 E L2 L3 S10' = maqa chi-DIR with a numerical affix ('strike / account-mark toward chi')
    L3 cord 5: 'E E E E L7' = qaqaqaqa-1OBJ ('my fourfold rock', +y? 1OBJ)
    L3 cord 6: 'E L2 L7' = TOP-chi-1OBJ ('the topic chi about me', +qa TOP + chi + 1OBJ)
  Of the 6 STRING L3 cords, 3 carry the -1OBJ suffix (-y / -wa = 'me / about me') -- matching the ORACULAR PERSONAL-VOICE signature of UR055 (Hair Khipu, ritual_oracle) where the consultant speaks in the first person.

VOCABULARY
  qaqa family (4 of 6 STRINGs): multi-qa compounds encoding either (a) topographic intensifiers for rocky landmarks at Chachapoyas, (b) emphatic topic reduplication (qa = topic marker; repetition = intensification), or (c) a NUMBERED HUACA naming system where qa-count identifies specific sacred rocks.
  chi (in 3 of 6 STRINGs): chi-compound TRUTH / verification marker (joins the chi-density cluster).
  maqa (1 of 6): strike / account-mark / tally-tick per polysemy rule.
  -1OBJ suffix (3 of 6): oracular personal voice ('me / about me').
  Total unique vocabulary: 5 plain words + 5 compound forms.
  v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE on this khipu (STRING onsets are L2 chi-, E qa-, L3 ma- -- none are L10 or L11).

VOCAB POSITIONS
  All 6 STRING cords are at L3 depth under L1 cord 7, L2 subsidiary 1. Position: deep buried in the tree, NOT in the main L1 sequence.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  W (white, 15 cords): primary magnitude channel in the numerical register. Carries cord 7 (val 759) which is the PARENT of the embedded oracle cell. W is both territorial-data here AND the HEADER CORD for the oracle consultation.
  AB (anonymous beige, 20 cords): secondary magnitude + the entire L3 narrative cell (6 of 6 L3 STRINGs are AB). AB is the MOST AMBIGUOUS color in the validated color-role table; here it serves as the TEXT channel for the embedded oracle.
  MB / KB / GG: peripheral data channels for the numerical register.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR015 is a LATE INCA TRIBUTE / CENSUS REGISTER from a Chachapoyas ayllu administered under the Inca conquest, with a BURIED RITUAL ORACLE consultation cell at depth L3.
  The main numerical register (clusters 2-9) records tribute or population counts at massive magnitudes (1400, 759, 2565, 2018, 1473, 1290, 1782, 1796, 1422, 500, 500, etc.) across multiple categories (W / AB / MB / KB / GG color channels).
  The L3 oracle cell (6 STRING cords under L1 cord 7) records a divinatory consultation about the tribute or about the ayllu's status, expressed in the canonical oracular voice: multi-qa rock-names (possibly naming the specific huacas being consulted -- 'the triple-rock', 'the sixfold rock', 'my fourfold rock'), the -DIR direction suffix ('toward'), the chi truth-verification marker, and the -1OBJ personal voice ('about me').
  The narrative reads as a ritual searching or request:
    (1) of the triple-truth-rock (CHIQAQAQA-GEN)
    (2) of the sixfold rock (QAQAQAQAQAQA)
    (3) toward the sixfold rock (QAQAQAQAQAQA-DIR)
    (4) strike / mark toward the truth (MAQA CHI-DIR)
    (5) my fourfold rock (QAQAQAQA-1OBJ)
    (6) the topic chi about me (TOP-CHI-1OBJ)
  This is CONSISTENT with an Inca-period Chachapoyas ORACLE CONSULTATION at a mountain huaca (qaqa-huaca, a rocky outcrop sacred site), asking about the consultant's own status. At Laguna de los Condores, the tomb-cave site where UR015 was recovered, ancestor oracular consultation was the documented ritual function (Schjellerup 2005, von Hagen 2012).
  The STRUCTURAL VARIANT here -- embedded oracle at L3 inside a numerical register -- is DIFFERENT from UR055 (Hair Khipu) where the oracle is the WHOLE document across 180 cords on LK text color. UR015 proposes that at the Chachapoyas highlands, oracle consultation was BURIED INTO administrative records rather than being a separate document class. Administrative accounting + ritual consultation co-existed in one khipu.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Cluster 0-1 (cords 1-2) [Opening silence]: 2 EMPTY cords bracket the document opening.
  Cluster 2 (cords 3-5) [Short W header]: 3 W cords with small values 70, 50, 10. Possibly a date triplet (C1=70 -> 1438+70=1508 CE, but C2=50 is not a valid month) -- more likely a category / flag triplet.
  Cluster 3 (cords 6-8) [LARGE W magnitudes]: 3 W cords with massive values 1400, 759, 90. CORD 7 (W 759) is the PARENT of the embedded oracle cell. The L2 subsidiary AB INT 13 under cord 7 carries the 7 L3 grandchildren (6 STRING + 1 INT = the ORACLE CELL).
  Cluster 4 (cords 9-39, 31 cords) [Main register detail]: 25 INT + 6 EMPTY on mixed colors (AB / BL / GG / KB / MB / W). Values spread 19 to 850 -- the document's main per-entity / per-period detail block.
  Cluster 5 (cords 40-43) [GG identity subtotals]: 4 cords mostly GG with values 630, 513, 537, 338. These may be IDENTITY-COLUMN subtotals for named entities (unnamed in this khipu; compare with UR123's GG chi-compound names).
  Cluster 6 (cords 44-51) [Mixed subtotals]: 8 cords carrying 430, 390, 539, 112, 430, 275, 485, 100.
  Cluster 7 (cords 52-53) [Small pair]: 2 cords 115 (GG) + 105 (KB).
  Cluster 8 (cords 54-60) [HUGE closing magnitudes]: 7 cords carrying 2565 (W), 500 + 500 (GG pair), 2018, 1473, 1290, 220 (MB chain). The 2565 on W is the document's PEAK magnitude.
  Cluster 9 (cords 61-64) [AB aggregate totals]: 4 cords 500 (GG) + 1782, 1796, 1422 (AB triplet). Possibly 3 major ayllu totals.
  Cluster 10 (cord 65) [Closing silence]: 1 EMPTY cord ends the document.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  The LAGUNA DE LOS CONDORES khipu assemblage (32 documents) was recovered in 1996-97 from the Pajaten cliff-tomb complex in Amazonas, Peru. The site is a shelf-tomb with Chachapoyas mummy bundles dated radiocarbon to the late 15th-early 16th century (von Hagen, Schjellerup). The khipus are attributed to the ayllus administered under the late Inca conquest of the Chachapoyas region (Tupac Yupanqui / Huayna Capac, ~1460-1532 CE).
  The Chachapoyas culture was distinct from the Inca (different language, material culture, architecture) and was conquered only in the final decades of the Inca empire. Their khipus likely record late-imperial tribute administration under Inca rule, in a HIGHLAND QUECHUA register distinct from the AYMARA-influenced coastal khipus (Pachacamac) we have been reading.
  Laguna de los Condores specifically was a MORTUARY / RITUAL site -- the dead were venerated as ancestors, and khipus deposited at such sites carried BOTH administrative records (tribute counts) AND oracular / ritual content (consultations about and with the dead). Documented Chachapoyas / Wari / Inca practice at similar sites (Zuidema, Ramirez).
  The MULTI-QA VOCABULARY may encode Chachapoyas-specific ritual geography: the region is dominated by QAQA (rocky cliffs and outcrops) -- the Andean cloud-forest topography. Each major rock could be a huaca with its own number-identity encoded in qa-count.

KEY PASSAGES
  L1 cord 7 (W 759): the PARENT HEADER of the embedded oracle cell. 759 units of something, with a deep-tree oracular consultation attached.
  L2 AB INT 13 (under cord 7): the IMMEDIATE PARENT of the 6 STRING L3 children. The value 13 may be a ritual index or the subsidiary's own tally.
  L3 cords 1-6 (the ORACLE CELL): 6 STRING cords forming a divinatory sequence in first-person oracular voice.
  Cluster 8 cord 54 (W 2565): the DOCUMENT'S PEAK MAGNITUDE -- the largest single value in 88 cords.
  Cluster 9 AB triplet (1782, 1796, 1422): three major ayllu closing totals.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  - The L3 STRING cords and their multi-qa / -1OBJ morphology: reproducible via syllabary v3 reader.
  - The structural placement (all 6 STRINGs under L1 cord 7 -> L2 subsidiary 1): reproducible from OKR.
  - Laguna de los Condores provenance: documented in Centro Mallqui archive and von Hagen / Schjellerup publications.
  - Chachapoyas late-Inca conquest period (1460-1532 CE): standard Inca chronology.
  - -1OBJ as oracular personal-voice signature: cross-validates with UR055 (Hair Khipu) where -wa suffix dominates.
  - Qaqa as huaca-rock terminology: widely documented (Cobo 1653, Salomon 1991).

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  No prior validated contribution exists for UR015. First deep read.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 88 cords, 11 clusters, 5 main colors, KFG twin KH0251.
  - Per OKR catalog: CMA-628/LC1-257F, Leymebamba, Centro Mallqui.
  - OKR note mentions subsidiary 5s4 / 5s5 being the two ends of a linked thread -- physical construction detail.
narrative:
A large account book. Eighty-eight cords, mostly numbers, in the mixed colors of Inca administrative tribute: white for magnitude, brown for detail, beige for identification, black-blue for separators. The values are enormous for a highland ayllu record -- fifteen hundred, two thousand, two thousand five hundred, seventeen hundred, seventeen hundred and ninety-six. These are population counts, or bundles of cloth, or units of maize, at the scale of an Inca census for the Chachapoyas region in the last generation of the empire.

But on cord seven, something unusual happens. The cord itself is white, carries the value seven hundred and fifty-nine, a mid-sized magnitude. Attached to it as a level-two subsidiary is a single beige cord valued thirteen. And attached to THAT, at level three -- deep in the subsidiary tree, where most khipu readers never look -- there are seven children. Six of them are STRING cords. Text. Words.

And the words are strange.

The first is chiqaqaqa-GEN -- 'of the triple-truth-rock.' A chi-compound, the truth-marker, attached to three figure-eights that give qa-qa-qa, and the genitive suffix -pa. Of the triple rock of truth.

The second is six figure-eights: qa-qa-qa-qa-qa-qa. The sixfold rock. Emphatic beyond emphatic.

The third is the same six figure-eights with an added L3-turn long-knot: qaqaqaqaqaqa-DIR. Toward the sixfold rock.

The fourth is maqa chi-DIR -- strike, then chi-toward, with a numerical affix S10. A motion, a striking, toward the truth.

The fifth is qa-qa-qa-qa plus L7: qaqaqaqa-1OBJ. My fourfold rock. The 1OBJ suffix -- the -wa, the -y -- says 'me / about me / mine.' The rock that belongs to me, or the rock that concerns me.

The sixth is the closing: E L2 L7. TOP-chi-1OBJ. The topic chi about me. The matter of truth, concerning me.

Six cords. Three of them with -1OBJ, the oracular personal voice. Four with multi-qa compounds naming rocks by the count of the figure-eights. Chi-compounds in three of the six, marking the truth-register throughout.

This is not accounting. This is CONSULTATION. A person brought this khipu to a huaca at Laguna de los Condores -- a cliff-tomb where the ancestors had been laid down in their mummy bundles a few generations earlier -- and asked a question. Which rock is my rock? Which truth concerns me? The numbers in the surrounding register count the tribute of the living; but the buried cord is about the living person who brought the record, asking the dead for guidance.

The sixfold rock. The fourfold rock. The triple-truth-rock. In Chachapoyas country -- the cloud-forest mountains, every cliff a potential huaca, every outcrop a potential ancestor -- the rocks were not generic; they were NUMBERED. Qaqa is rock. Qaqaqa is the triple rock. Qaqaqaqa is the fourfold rock. Qaqaqaqaqaqa is the sixfold rock. Perhaps each rock-name was a specific huaca at a specific altitude along a specific ridge, identifiable by anyone who knew the territory by its number of qa-repetitions encoded in cotton.

And back at the main register -- cord seven on white, value 759 -- the consultation cell sits attached like a footnote. The scribe who wove this khipu knew that the tribute and the oracle belonged together. The living and the dead, counted and consulted, in one cotton document. Eighty-eight cords. One ritual cell buried at level three. An Inca census and a Chachapoyas oracle, woven into a single fabric, stored in a mummy bundle until 1996 when they were found -- still intact, still readable, still asking on the scribe's behalf: which is MY rock, which is the truth about me?

prior_work.claim: The Laguna de los Condores khipu assemblage (32 khipus) was recovered in 1996-97 from a Chachapoyas cliff-tomb in Amazonas, Peru, and dated to the late Inca period (~1460-1532 CE).
prior_work.citation: von Hagen, A. (2012). The Chachapoya. In: Handbook of South American Archaeology. Springer; Schjellerup, I. (2005). Reciprocidad y Conflicto: Los Chachapoyas en el Tiempo de los Incas. IFEA, Lima.

prior_work.claim: Chachapoyas culture was distinct from Inca (different language, material culture) and was conquered only in the final decades of the empire under Tupac Yupanqui and Huayna Capac.
prior_work.citation: Nair, S. & Langlois, S. (2017). The Chachapoyas: Warriors of the Clouds. Archaeology Magazine; Cieza de Leon (1553). Cronica del Peru, Parte I.

prior_work.claim: qaqa = rock / mountain / sacred stone is a widely attested Quechua term and a core element of Andean huaca vocabulary. Mountain-outcrop huacas at tomb-cave sites were consulted as ancestors.
prior_work.citation: Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo, Book 13; Salomon, F. (1991). The Huarochiri Manuscript; Zuidema, R.T. (1964). The Ceque System of Cuzco.

prior_work.claim: The -1OBJ / -wa personal-voice suffix is documented in UR055 (Hair Khipu, ritual_oracle singleton, Pachacamac, 180 cords, 94 percent LK) as the signature of an ORACULAR consultation where the consultant speaks in the first person.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). UR055 deep read -- succession oracle. ALBA Project validated contribution.

v3_contribution.claim: First deep read of UR015. PROPOSED CLASSIFICATION: ritual_oracle with a NOVEL STRUCTURAL VARIANT -- the oracle consultation is EMBEDDED at depth L3 as 6 STRING grandchildren of a single L2 subsidiary, rather than dominating the whole document (as in UR055's 180-cord whole-document oracle).

evidence.test: All 6 STRING cords localized under one L2 parent at L3 depth

v3_contribution.claim: PROMOTES ritual_oracle from singleton (UR055 only) to N=2 in the validated corpus -- but with a structural distinction: UR055 = dominant whole-document oracle, UR015 = embedded buried oracle cell. Suggests ritual_oracle has at least TWO structural variants.

MULTI-QA VOCABULARY SYSTEM HYPOTHESIS: the 4 qaqa-variants (qaqaqa, qaqaqaqa, qaqaqaqaqaqa appearing 2x) may encode a NUMBERED-HUACA naming system where qa-count identifies specific rocky huacas along Chachapoyas ridges (triple-rock, fourfold-rock, sixfold-rock). Alternative: emphatic topic reduplication where qa=TOP and qa x N = extreme emphasis.

evidence.test: 4 distinct qa-count compounds in 6 STRING cords, Chachapoyas topography context

v3_contribution.claim: ORACULAR PERSONAL-VOICE SIGNATURE: 3 of 6 STRING cords carry -1OBJ suffix (my / about me), matching the UR055 -wa dominant pattern. The buried cell reads as a CONSULTATION QUERY about the consultant's own truth / rock / status.

evidence.test: -1OBJ suffix on 3 of 6 STRING cords = 50 percent personal-voice density in the text cell

v3_contribution.claim: HIGHLAND CHACHAPOYAS LINGUISTIC REGISTER: UR015 is the first VALIDATED HIGHLAND (Chachapoyas / Leymebamba) khipu in the session. Vocabulary (multi-qa, chi-compounds, -1OBJ) differs from the coastal Pachacamac khipus (which used taki/paki ritual vocab, pama formula, commodity polysemy). Highland oracle register uses INTENSIFIED ROCK-NAMING unique to this region.
v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE on this khipu (STRING onsets are L2 chi-, E qa-, L3 ma- -- none are L10 or L11). Confirms v3.1 non-perturbation on a ritual_oracle document at a new provenance.

companions.note: Pachacamac ritual_oracle (Hair Khipu, 180 cords, 94 percent LK). UR015 promotes UR055 singleton to N=2 but with a structural inversion: UR055 is dominant whole-document oracle; UR015 is embedded-at-L3 oracle cell inside a numerical register. Both share the -1OBJ personal-voice signature that marks oracle consultations.

Pachacamac juridical_oracle (98 cords, BCRP). Also uses the canuto construction + phase separators + interrogative patterns, but judicial rather than ritual register. UR015 extends the ORACLE genre family to Chachapoyas highlands.

Sister Leymebamba khipu (572 cords, same Centro Mallqui / Laguna de los Condores collection). UR001 is much larger and presumably similar-function -- worth a deep read to test if UR001 also has embedded L3 oracle cells.
Leymebamba sister (80 cords, 6 STRING, CMA 480 catalog). Should be deep-read next to test replication of the embedded-oracle pattern within the Laguna de los Condores corpus.
Leymebamba sister (126 cords, 5 STRING, CMA-628 catalog like UR015). CMA-628 may be a coherent sub-collection within the Mallqui archive; UR012 and UR015 could be functionally related within it.
Pachacamac ceque_registry (huaca listing). UR015's multi-qa rock-naming system may be the HIGHLAND analog of UR203's coastal ceque-organized huaca registry.
Pachacamac lineage_land_registry with 7 chi-compound hanan-hurin names. UR015 shares the chi-compound truth-naming convention but in a different (oracular) register.
British Museum nomination_ceremony with 12 chi-compound truth-names. UR015 joins the chi-density cluster with 3 chi-compounds in its 6-STRING oracle cell.

word_reading.note: 6/6 STRING cords structurally decoded; compound morphology (multi-qa + chi- + -DIR / -GEN / -1OBJ) is fully analysable. 3/6 base words in dict; the multi-qa chains are reduplicative compounds not explicitly in the plain dictionary but morphologically transparent.

document_type.note: ritual_oracle classification rests on: (1) -1OBJ personal-voice signature (3/6 STRINGs) matching UR055 convention, (2) chi-compound truth-naming in the oracle cell, (3) multi-qa rock/huaca vocabulary consistent with Chachapoyas oracular geography, (4) Laguna de los Condores tomb-cave provenance (ancestor consultation documented), (5) embedded structure at L3 as a novel variant. Alternative classifications: administrative_record with ritual annotation (reduces the oracle to a footnote), or a novel 'embedded_oracle' sub-genre. Medium confidence reflects that this is only the SECOND ritual_oracle and the first embedded-variant -- needs UR007 / UR012 / UR001 replication.

narrative.note: Internally coherent: the numerical register + embedded oracle cell reading is plausible for a Chachapoyas ayllu khipu under late Inca administration at a mortuary site. Externally anchored by Schjellerup 2005 and von Hagen 2012 on Laguna de los Condores, and by UR055's -1OBJ oracle convention. The MULTI-QA HUACA-NAMING interpretation is speculative but grounded in Chachapoyas topography and Andean qa = rock-huaca vocabulary.

data_integrity.note: KFG twin KH0251, 0 Ascher sums declared (LOW / NONE integrity tier -- expected for a document that is not a commodity audit). All cord values clean, no L?, no DIVERGENT. OKR investigator note about physical construction on cord 5 subsidiaries is preserved; no damage notes affecting data.

sections.title: Opening Silence
sections.reading: Two EMPTY cords open the document. Standard bracketing-silence convention observed across ritual / ceremonial khipus.

sections.title: Short W Header
sections.reading: Three W cords carrying small values 70, 50, 10. Possibly a category / flag triplet (not a standard date).

sections.title: LARGE W Magnitudes + EMBEDDED ORACLE CELL
sections.reading: Three W cords carrying huge magnitudes 1400, 759, 90. CORD 7 (W 759) is the PARENT HEADER of the embedded oracle cell: under cord 7 there is a single L2 child (AB INT 13) which holds 7 L3 grandchildren, of which 6 are STRING forming the oracular consultation sequence.

key_cords.gloss: W INT 759 -> L2 AB 13 -> 6 L3 STRINGs
key_cords.note: PARENT of the embedded oracle cell. The L3 narrative reads: chiqaqaqa-GEN / qaqaqaqaqaqa / qaqaqaqaqaqa-DIR / maqa chi-DIR / qaqaqaqa-1OBJ / TOP-chi-1OBJ

sections.title: Main Register Detail
sections.reading: 31 cords of main register detail across 5 colors (AB / BL / GG / KB / MB / W). 25 INT + 6 EMPTY. Values range 19 to 850 -- the per-entity / per-period detail of the tribute / census register.

sections.title: GG Identity Subtotals + Mixed Sub-Totals
sections.reading: Cluster 5 (cords 40-43): 4 mostly-GG cords carrying 630, 513, 537, 338 -- possibly identity-column subtotals for named entities. Cluster 6 (cords 44-51): 8 mixed-color cords 430 / 390 / 539 / 112 / 430 / 275 / 485 / 100.

sections.title: Closing Huge Magnitudes
sections.reading: Cluster 7 (cords 52-53): small pair 115 GG + 105 KB. Cluster 8 (cords 54-60): 7 cords with HUGE values 2565 (W, PEAK), 500+500 (GG), 2018, 1473, 1290, 220 (MB chain). Cluster 9 (cords 61-64): 500 (GG) + 1782, 1796, 1422 (AB triplet). Probably 3 major ayllu aggregate totals.

sections.title: Closing Silence
sections.reading: Single EMPTY cord closes the document.
